summary: Frontend testing of three new Ecosystem destination pages (/ecosystem/events, /ecosystem/news, /ecosystem/certifications) and SPA navigation from the Ecosystem hub. All 8 acceptance criteria pass. Hero is dark navy (#0A1628 confirmed via computed style rgb(10,22,40)), breadcrumb 'Home > Ecosystem > <Section>' renders correctly with teal Ecosystem link, section headers ('All events', 'All news', 'All certifications') present, news page hydrates 50 RSS stories, events and certifications gracefully show empty state in this preview env. All three hub 'View all' links navigate via SPA (window marker preserved -> no full reload). Breadcrumb 'Home' and 'Ecosystem' links also use SPA navigation.
critical_code_review_comments: All three subpages follow same boilerplate (api fetch + loading/empty/list states). Consider extracting a small `useEcosystemList(endpoint)` hook to DRY ~15 lines per page if more sections are added.
EcosystemSubpageHero takes `current` as a string but does not allow customization of the hero background or accent — acceptable for now since spec mandates uniform styling.
Events/Certifications endpoints return empty arrays in preview env; ensure backend seeds or RSS hydration is set up before production. Empty-state UX is good.
test_credentials: Not required — all pages are public.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three ecosystem subpages render with correct data-testids: ecosystem-events-page, ecosystem-news-page, ecosystem-certifications-page, ecosystem-subpage-hero, ecosystem-subpage-breadcrumb, events-list/empty, news-list, news-count (shows '50 stories'), certs-list/empty. SPA navigation verified from hub view-all links (events-section-header-view-all, news-section-header-view-all, certs-section-header-view-all) and from breadcrumb Home/Ecosystem links. Events and certifications backends return [] in preview env — empty state UI works as designed.
